scheme_name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Plant protection pesticides Gujarat
page_title: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Plant protection pesticides Gujarat

details: The scheme "Distribution of Seeds of more productive varieties / Hybrid varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies, etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area (TASP)" with a component "Plant protection pesticides" is introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to help Scheduled Tribe farmers and tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas achieve higher agricultural crop production and improve their livelihood. Through this scheme, assistance is provided for various components, including plant protection pesticides, which, when used according to scientific recommendations, can significantly increase crop production.
benefits: 75% of the purchase price or ₹ 750/- per hectare, whichever is lower (subject to a maximum of two hectares per account)
: According to conditions No. 5, 16, 17, 19 and 22 of Appendix-1, crop protection drugs included under Pesticides Act-1968 have to be given.
Biological/herbal medicines are to be preferred.
eligibility: The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe.
The applicant should be a farmer.
The applicant should be located in tribal areas OR be a tribal cultivator residing outside tribal areas.
The scheme excludes farmers from Dang district.

offline: Approach the Gram Sevak in your village for access to "Plant protection pesticides" under the scheme.

faqs.question: Can I receive a subsidy for using plant protection pesticides on my farm?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can receive a subsidy for using plant protection pesticides. The assistance is provided to help increase agricultural production.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on the number of hectares for which I can receive the subsidy?
faqs.answer: Yes, the subsidy for plant protection pesticides is capped at a maximum of two hectares per account.

faqs.question: Do I have to use a specific type of pesticide to be eligible for the subsidy?
faqs.answer: Yes, the crop protection drugs must be those included under the Pesticides Act-1968.

faqs.question: Are there any preferred types of pesticides under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme gives preference to biological and herbal medicines for crop protection.

faqs.question: How often can I claim the subsidy for plant protection pesticides?
faqs.answer: You can claim the subsidy for plant protection pesticides every year.

faqs.question: Does the scheme cover other farming inputs besides pesticides?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme also provides assistance for other items such as hybrid seeds and fertilizers.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Scheduled Tribe farmers are eligible to apply for the benefits of this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the main objective of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The main objective is to help Scheduled Tribe farmers and tribal cultivators increase their agricultural production and improve their livelihood.

faqs.question: What kind of farming practices are encouraged under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme encourages the use of various tools and practices, including plant protection equipment, field demonstrations, and micronutrients, based on scientific recommendations.

faqs.question: Does the scheme apply to tribal cultivators living outside the tribal areas?
faqs.answer: Yes, tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas are also eligible to receive benefits under the scheme.
